---
title: "Human Intent as a Formal Input"
chapter_id: "human-intent-as-a-formal-input"
part_id: "foundations-alignment-governance"
status: "conceptual"
last_updated: "2026-06-24"
primary_sources:
  - "viea"
  - "software_magic_grimoire"
  - "planforge"
  - "cognitive_compilation"
  - "talos"
evidence_level: "argument"
claim_label: "Design rationale"
open_evidence_gaps:
  - "Assigned source texts have not yet been ingested or summarized."
  - "No Codex tests have been implemented or run."
  - "Chapter prose is a scaffold, not a completed manuscript draft."
---

# Human Intent as a Formal Input

## Chapter status

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Chapter ID | `human-intent-as-a-formal-input` |
| Part | Part I - Foundations, Alignment, and Governance |
| Status | conceptual |
| Last updated | 2026-06-24 |
| Primary source records | `viea`, `software_magic_grimoire`, `planforge`, `cognitive_compilation`, `talos` |
| Claim label | Design rationale |
| Evidence level | argument |
| Source ingestion state | Source records assigned; source texts not yet ingested. |
| Test state | Planned only; no tests have been run. |

## Drafting guardrail

This stub is derived from `book_structure.json` and the source inventory only. It does not claim that the listed source documents have been ingested, summarized, or independently verified.

## Problem

A governed stack must start from human intent without letting natural-language ambiguity become unrestricted execution authority.

## Why existing approaches are insufficient

Prompt-only workflows blur request, requirements, permission, risk tolerance, and acceptance criteria.

## Core Claim

[human-intent-as-a-formal-input.core, label: Design rationale, support: argument] Human intent should enter the stack as a structured contract with goals, constraints, authority, artifacts, evidence requirements, and stop conditions.

## Mechanism

- Parse intent into command contracts.
- Separate desired outcome from allowed means.
- Attach acceptance criteria and escalation conditions.

## Interfaces

- Alignment filters intent.
- Planning compiles accepted contracts.
- Execution consumes only authorized task contracts.

## Invariants

- Ambiguity triggers clarification or bounded defaults.
- Intent contracts expose authority limits.
- Stop conditions remain attached to the plan.

## Failure modes

- Goal misbinding.
- Scope creep.
- Implicit permission to act.

## Minimal implementation

An intent-contract schema and example transformations from request to governed task.

## Codex test plan

| Test | Purpose | Status |
|---|---|---|
| Intent parsing ambiguity test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Authority extraction test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Stop-condition preservation test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |

## Source crosswalk

| Source ID | Title | Planned use |
|---|---|---|
| `viea` | Verified Intent-to-Execution Architecture | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Keystone source. Human intent -> command contracts -> artifacts -> routing -> runtime targets -> verification -> deployment -> feedback. |
| `software_magic_grimoire` | Software Magic Grimoire | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Structured command contracts and semantic density of software words. |
| `planforge` | PlanForge | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Planning substrate. Goal-to-execution compilation, hierarchical decomposition, DAG planning, scheduling, intelligence arbitrage. |
| `cognitive_compilation` | Cognitive Compilation | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Compiler framing for LLM-centered planning, semantic IR, target compilation, incremental repair. |
| `talos` | Talos Protocol | Planned use from inventory/manifest: AI labor OS. Deterministic cognitive manufacturing, typed jobs, control planes, auditability, tool isolation. |

## Summary

This chapter currently establishes the structural slot for this layer of the ASI stack. The next drafting pass should ingest assigned sources, separate source-derived claims from design hypotheses, and update Appendix C when support states change.
